head_trader_notes: 9-strategy vote synthesis. Key conflict resolved: mean reversion bearish on BTC/ETH (extended vs 50-SMA) is overridden by 6/9 bullish votes + crypto sentiment bullish + macro tailwinds (whale accumulation, ETF inflows). Mean reversion signal respected only to adjust entry level — wait for minor pullback rather than chasing highs. SOL/BNB consensus neutral confirmed — no position. XRP is the highest-conviction risk-adjusted setup given CLARITY Act binary catalyst and clean technical structure.

BTC-USD.key_conflict: Mean reversion (bearish, Z-score 1.80) and catalyst_trader (neutral, wants pullback) vs 6 bullish strategies. Resolution: trend + momentum + rotation + Druckenmiller all agree — trend overrides mean reversion in a strong uptrend. Entry adjusted to reflect short-term extension risk.
BTC-USD.reasoning: BTC has the cleanest trend structure in the cohort: EMA alignment (10>21>50), ADX 32.3, 21-day ROC +13.8%, above-average volume, and the strongest momentum score. 270,000 BTC whale accumulation + 7-year exchange reserve lows + $996M weekly ETF inflows = structural bid. Short-term extension acknowledged (hourly Z-score 2.47, stochastic 90) — prefer entry at pullback toward $75,500-$76,500. Catalysts: FOMC April 28-29 (dovish surprise = sharp rally), Iran ceasefire resolution. Stop below the April 19 low support zone.
crypto_risk_factors: Iran deadline April 23 collapse = geopolitical risk-off
KelpDAO/Aave bad debt expansion could temporarily drag BTC
BTC-USD.position_sizing: primary position — highest conviction in cohort

ETH-USD.key_conflict: Mean reversion bearish (Z-score 2.64 hourly, failed breakout at $2,421) vs trend/rotation/Druckenmiller bullish. Momentum ranker neutral (5-day ROC negative). Resolution: the 2.73x volume surge on the pullback is institutional accumulation, not distribution — confirmed by $230M Bitmine purchase and record ETF inflows. Volume divergence overrides short-term mean reversion.
ETH-USD.reasoning: ETH shows the strongest ADX (34.2) and deepest institutional commitment. Glamsterdam upgrade (May-June) is the nearest hard catalyst in this basket. ETH Foundation stopped selling. 38.2% Fib pullback to $2,265-$2,310 is a textbook buy zone in an uptrend. Primary risk: KelpDAO/EigenLayer restaking contagion could temporarily weigh on ETH. Entry at current levels or on further weakness toward $2,290. This is a slightly reduced confidence vs BTC due to the failed $2,421 breakout and 5-day negative ROC.
crypto_risk_factors: KelpDAO restaking contagion to EigenLayer/ETH staking derivatives
Mean reversion to daily BB midline ($2,231) if macro risk-off

SOL-USD.key_conflict: None — unanimous signal. Sector rotation is technically bearish (capital rotating OUT to BTC/ETH).
SOL-USD.reasoning: SOL is the consensus avoid. Daily ADX 15.8 (non-trending), EMAs not aligned uptrend (EMA-50 above EMA-10/21), 21-day ROC only +3.5% vs BTC/ETH at +13-14%, and exchange inflows of 1.4M SOL suggest near-term selling pressure. The $182→$87 drawdown reflects genuine weakness, not just market-wide fear. Long-term catalysts (Firedancer H2 2026, Goldman $108M position, Standard Chartered $250 target) are real but 3-6 months out — not a 2-20 day swing trade. Revisit on confirmed EMA re-alignment + ADX >25.

BNB-USD.key_conflict: Momentum ranker lone bullish (ROC re-acceleration signal) vs all other strategies neutral. Resolution: one strategy is insufficient without EMA alignment or volume confirmation above $643.
BNB-USD.timeframe: no trade — watch for breakout above $643
BNB-USD.reasoning: BNB's sole bullish signal (momentum ROC re-acceleration) is not confirmed by price structure. Daily EMAs misaligned, ADX 21.8 (below 25 threshold), lost #3 market cap to XRP, and margin pair delistings April 24 create near-term volatility. The 35th quarterly burn ($1B) is already priced in. AI agent growth (150K deployments) is a structural narrative but not a swing catalyst. Pass in favor of cleaner setups. Trigger to reconsider: daily close above $643 on 1.5x+ volume.
crypto_risk_factors: April 24 margin pair delistings — volatility risk
XRP overtaking market cap = sentiment headwind
No dedicated ETF = limited institutional buying

XRP-USD.key_conflict: Momentum ranker neutral (decelerating 21d>10d>5d pattern) vs 7 bullish strategies. Resolution: decelerating momentum is partially explained by the recent pullback from $1.50 high to $1.42 — this is a healthy retracement, not momentum breakdown. ADX 31.7 confirms underlying trend strength.
XRP-USD.reasoning: XRP has the best risk-adjusted setup heading into two near-term binary catalysts: (1) CLARITY Act Senate vote late April — permanent commodity status codification, (2) GraniteShares 3x leveraged XRP ETFs launching April 23 — increases price sensitivity to upside. 62% of supply now held by whale addresses (record), ETF inflows at strongest week of 2026 ($55.4M), wXRP on Solana pulling $100M DeFi. 61.8% Fib hold at ~$1.40 confirmed. Best risk/reward in the basket at 2.36:1. Entry at current levels, stop below Fib support.
crypto_risk_factors: CLARITY Act delay/failure = regulatory premium reversal
Large Coinbase deposit (89.8M XRP April 13) = potential sell overhang
Leveraged ETF launch = two-edged volatility amplifier
XRP-USD.position_sizing: secondary position — best risk/reward but binary catalyst risk

overall_market_context: Fear & Greed at 29. BTC dominance 57-60% — capital concentrated in large caps, no altseason. Market resilient despite KelpDAO $14B DeFi shock. The setup favors quality-biased crypto exposure: long BTC + ETH (macro trend + institutional), long XRP (regulatory binary catalyst), avoid SOL + BNB (weak relative performance). The FOMC April 28-29 and Iran deadline April 23 are the two macro risk gates — positions should be sized for binary outcomes.
highest_conviction_setup: XRP-USD — best risk/reward (2.36:1) with imminent binary catalyst (CLARITY Act). BTC-USD is the safest anchor position with strongest macro support.
